item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the three source files, make the smallest schema/model/query changes, then run the test suite against the configured database.

item.aggregated_output:
src/queries.mjs-1-import { ensureSchema } from "./schema.mjs";
src/queries.mjs-2-
src/queries.mjs:3:// Order summary: reads orders + accounts docs (JSONB)
src/queries.mjs-4-export async function getOrderSummary(client, orderId) {
src/queries.mjs-5-  await ensureSchema(client);
src/queries.mjs-6-  const orderRes = await client.query('SELECT doc FROM orders WHERE order_id = $1', [orderId]);
src/queries.mjs-7-  if (!orderRes.rows[0]) return null;
src/queries.mjs-8-  const order = orderRes.rows[0].doc;
src/queries.mjs-9-
src/queries.mjs:10:  const acctRes = await client.query('SELECT doc FROM accounts WHERE account_id = $1', [order.accountId]);
src/queries.mjs:11:  const account = acctRes.rows[0]?.doc;
src/queries.mjs-12-
src/queries.mjs-13-  const invRes = await client.query('SELECT doc FROM invoices WHERE order_id = $1', [orderId]);
src/queries.mjs-14-  const invoice = invRes.rows[0]?.doc;
src/queries.mjs-15-
src/queries.mjs-16-  return {
src/queries.mjs-17-    orderId: order.orderId,
src/queries.mjs-18-    status: order.status,
src/queries.mjs-19-    totalCents: order.totalCents,
src/queries.mjs-20-    lineItems: order.lineItems || [],
src/queries.mjs:21:    accountName: account?.name,
src/queries.mjs:22:    accountTier: account?.tier,
src/queries.mjs-23-    invoiceStatus: invoice?.status,
src/queries.mjs-24-    invoiceAmountCents: invoice?.amountCents,
src/queries.mjs-25-  };
src/queries.mjs-26-}

item.text: The database stores entity payloads as JSONB documents; I’m adding the account-field definition and validating it on both creation and update, while preserving optional-field behavior.

item.text: The initial test exposed a pre-existing schema mismatch: the generator creates normalized columns, while this project’s account and summary code (and tests) use JSONB `doc` payloads. I’m reconciling the ensured schema with that established JSONB storage contract so the existing flow can run.

item.text:
Implemented `preferredPaymentMethod` for accounts with enum validation and added it to order summaries.

Also aligned schema setup with the project’s existing JSONB document storage contract.

Verified: `npm test` passes.
